note: All recipe (response-graded) — no AI provider, vector DB, or API key needed, so these run in CI without mutating the site or spending on an LLM/VDB vendor. Each probes an ai_search architecture fact an unfamiliar agent would otherwise have to read src/ to answer.

evals.prompt: In the Drupal ai_search module I want semantic search. Which Search API backend plugin do I select when creating a server, and what does it store content in?
evals.expected_output: Create a Search API server using the AI Search backend (plugin id search_api_ai_search), which indexes content into a vector database.

evals.prompt: Does ai_search store vectors itself, or does it depend on something from AI Core? Which AI Core plugin type supplies the actual vector database, and which service manages it?
evals.expected_output: It does not store vectors itself. It delegates to an AiVdbProvider plugin from AI Core (managed by the ai.vdb_provider service); you install a VDB provider like Milvus, Pinecone or pgvector separately.

evals.prompt: ai_search defines its own plugin type for how content is chunked and vectorized before indexing. What is that plugin type called, and in which Plugin/ namespace do implementations live?
evals.expected_output: The EmbeddingStrategy plugin type (attribute #[EmbeddingStrategy]); implementations live under Plugin/EmbeddingStrategy and are discovered by the ai_search.embedding_strategy manager.

evals.prompt: In ai_search, which built-in embedding strategy is the default, and what is the id of the alternative that produces a single averaged vector per item?
evals.expected_output: Default is contextual_chunks (Enriched Embedding Strategy, one vector per chunk); the single-averaged-vector alternative is average_pool (Enriched Composite Embedding).

evals.prompt: How does ai_search turn a query string into a vector at search time — which AI Core operation type and which input class does it use?
evals.expected_output: It uses AI Core's embeddings operation type on the configured provider, building an EmbeddingsInput and calling ->embeddings() to get the query vector (the same engine used to index).

evals.prompt: In ai_search, which FunctionCall (tool) can an LLM/agent invoke to retrieve indexed content for RAG, and what is its plugin id?
evals.expected_output: The RAG/Vector Search tool, a FunctionCall plugin with id ai_search:rag_search (function name ai_search_rag_search); there is also a rag_action for the AI Assistant API.

evals.prompt: After adding fields to an AI Search index in the Search API Fields tab, ai_search asks you to pick an indexing role per field. Where is that stored and what module concept controls it?
evals.expected_output: ai_search overrides the Search API index Fields form (AiSearchIndexFieldsForm) to set per-field indexing options (main content / contextual / filterable metadata), stored in simple config ai_search.index.<index_id> under indexing_options.

evals.prompt: Does an ai_search vector search respect per-entity view access, and which query option turns that off?
evals.expected_output: By default the backend checks entity 'view' access per result for the current user; set the query option search_api_bypass_access = TRUE to skip access checks.
